metadata.title: Claude Wrote a C Compiler From Scratch: What That Actually Proves.

skin_warnings: B06: palette=claude but the outro is 'LogoOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · Claude Wrote a C Compiler From Scratch: What That Actually Proves.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. A C compiler is a multi-stage pipeline — lexer, parser, AST, SSA, optimizer, code generator, assembler, linker — each its own subsystem. Claude built every stage from scratch for four architectures with no external toolchain dependencies. The method is the finding: humans write only tests, and Claude diagnoses failing tests and iterates without hints. Its README warns the code is unvalidated — building something you shouldn't deploy still proves the model can build it.

artifactLines: A C compiler is a multi-stage pipeline — lexer, parser, AST, SSA, optimizer, code generator, assembler, linker — each its own subsystem.
Claude built every stage from scratch for four architectures with no external toolchain dependencies.
The method is the finding: humans write only tests, and Claude diagnoses failing tests and iterates without hints.
Its README warns the code is unvalidated — building something you shouldn't deploy still proves the model can build it.

beats.narration_text: Here is why Claude Wrote a C Compiler From Scratch: What That Actually Proves. belongs in your working knowledge. The systems, policies, and tools shaped by it affect decisions at your level — whether or not you understand the mechanism. Knowing the underlying logic gives you the frame; missing it means reasoning from output with no access to the inputs.

artifactLines: A C compiler is a multi-stage pipeline — lexer, parser, AST, SSA, optimizer, code generator, assembler, linker — each its own subsystem.
Claude built every stage from scratch for four architectures with no external toolchain dependencies.
The method is the finding: humans write only tests, and Claude diagnoses failing tests and iterates without hints.

beats.narration_text: The line that grabs me is 'the methodology is the finding, not the artifact.' I want to run a smaller version of that experiment myself. Help me (1) pick a subsystem — say a lexer and parser — and set up the rule where I only write tests and Claude does everything else with no debugging help, (2) design a failing test that forces Claude to diagnose and iterate alone, and (3) decide what my own 'do not use this code' caveat should honestly say afterward.

props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · BUILD WITH CLAUDE · ANTHROPIC
props.segment: Claude Wrote a C Compiler From Scratch: What That Actually Proves.
props.command: The line that grabs me is 'the methodology is the finding, not the artifact.' I want to run a smaller version of that experiment myself. Help me (1) pick a subsystem — say a lexer and parser — and set up the rule where I only write tests and Claude does everything else with no debugging help, (2) design a failing test that forces Claude to diagnose and iterate alone, and (3) decide what my own 'do not use this code' caveat should honestly say afterward.

beats.narration_text: A C compiler is not a single function. It is a six-stage pipeline: Lexer, Parser, AST, SSA intermediate representation, Optimizer, Code Generator, Assembler, Linker. Each stage is its own subsystem. No external toolchain dependencies — every stage written from scratch. Four target architectures: x86-64, i686, AArch64, RISC-V.

graphic.production_viz: Eight-stage pipeline: each stage lights up left-to-right as a block animation. No dependency arrows.

beats.narration_text: The methodology is the finding, not the artifact. One rule: humans write tests, Claude writes everything else — and never gets debugging help. When Claude gets stuck, it reads the failing test, diagnoses, and iterates. No pair-programming. No hints. The human's only input after setup is the test suite. The constraint is the method.

graphic.production_viz: Two-box asymmetry: Human writes (tests only) vs Claude writes (everything, iterates against tests)

beats.narration_text: The README says, verbatim: 'I do not recommend you use this code — none of it has been validated for correctness.' That caveat is the Teardown line. A model that can build something it should not be deployed to run is still a model that can build it. The compiler compiles C and generates ELF executables. That is real. Whether you'd trust it in production is a separate judgment.

graphic.production_viz: Quote card with README caveat + two-column: what it proved vs what it did not prove

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude: 'Design a test suite for a minimal C compiler — just enough to drive TDD on lexing, parsing, and code generation for a hello-world program.' That prompt puts you at the start of the methodology. What does the test surface look like before a single line of compiler code exists? Run it. Liam, in for Bear.
